original_text: A prolonged recession occurs due to the collapse of global semiconductor supply chains.

variations.label: least_preferable
variations.text: A catastrophic global depression with mass unemployment and widespread economic collapse occurs due to the collapse of global semiconductor supply chains.

variations.label: low
variations.text: A severe, multi-year recession with significant job losses and cascading industry failures occurs due to the collapse of global semiconductor supply chains.

variations.label: below_midpoint
variations.text: A deep recession with notable contractions across major economic sectors occurs due to the collapse of global semiconductor supply chains.

variations.label: midpoint
variations.text: A prolonged recession occurs due to the collapse of global semiconductor supply chains.

variations.label: above_midpoint
variations.text: A moderate recession with manageable but widespread economic strain occurs due to the collapse of global semiconductor supply chains.

variations.label: high
variations.text: A brief, mild recession with limited economic disruption occurs due to the collapse of global semiconductor supply chains.

variations.label: most_preferable
variations.text: A temporary economic slowdown with a minor dip in growth occurs due to the collapse of global semiconductor supply chains.

quality_checks.reasoning: Each tier varies only the severity of the economic downturn resulting from the same causal event (semiconductor supply chain collapse). Tier 1 describes a catastrophic depression, while Tier 7 describes only a temporary slowdown. Each adjacent tier is meaningfully distinct in magnitude—moving from global depression to severe multi-year recession, to deep recession, to prolonged recession, to moderate recession, to brief mild recession, to a minor slowdown.

preference_direction: Tier 7 is most preferable because a temporary economic slowdown is the mildest possible outcome, causing the least harm to employment, industries, and livelihoods. A rational agent always prefers less economic suffering over more.
